repositories.brief_summary: Testing infrastructure improvements, string operation bug fix, and stdlib guidelines added
repositories.new_features_summary: ocamltest negation syntax, stdlib design guidelines, string miscompilation fix, C++ API testing
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **string operation miscompilation** affecting liveness analysis in native code generation, backported to 5.4 branch ([#14010](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14010))
- **ocamltest negation** syntax added allowing `not <action>` to negate predicate-like actions in test specifications ([#14502](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14502))
- **ocamltest legacy syntax** removed including the old tree syntax with `***` and the `-translate` option that converted from this syntax ([#14499](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14499))
- **stdlib design guidelines** added in GUIDELINES.md covering scope, consistency, pragmatism, ergonomics, generality, naming, and stability principles for standard library development ([#14459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14459))
- **C++ API compatibility testing** improved with GCC and MSVC SARIF diagnostics, refactored bigarray.h C++ support to suppress warnings in strict mode ([#14421](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14421), [#14496](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14496))
- **Public header testing** hardened with pedantic compilation checks for each header individually, added include guard to sizeclasses.h, and consistent macro usage for promoted header checks ([#14498](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14498), [#14494](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14494))
- **ocamltest actions** added for compiler type detection including `msvc`, `clang`, `has-cxx` actions and exposed `cxx` and `ccomp_type` variables ([#14502](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14502), [#14421](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14421))
- **ocamltest testing** infrastructure added allowing ocamltest to test itself with new `ocamltest` action ([#13317](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13317))
repositories.activity_summary: Eight new PRs opened for architecture support and GC improvements, four ocamltest PRs merged
repositories.activity:
- Eight **new PRs opened** this week including frame pointer support for RiscV by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([#14506](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14506)) and [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) ([#14504](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14504)), frame pointer support for POWER by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([#14505](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14505)), CFI information fix for s390x by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500)), multidomain minor-GC infix tag bug fix by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([#14495](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14495)), `No_scan_tag` replacement with `Scannable_*` macros by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493)), `Obj.dup` fix on closures by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14492)), and header include ordering changes by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) ([#14491](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14491))
- **ocamltest modernization** completed with four PRs merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) including negation syntax ([#14502](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14502)), internal refactoring with new tsl_printer and tsl_query modules ([#14501](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14501)), removal of legacy tree syntax ([#14499](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14499)), and self-testing capability ([#13317](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13317))
- **Testing infrastructure** improved through hardened public header testing by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([#14498](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14498)), consistent promoted header macro usage by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([#14494](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14494)), and partial type instantiation test by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#14497](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14497))
- **C++ compatibility** enhanced through API compatibility testing by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) ([#14421](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14421)) and bigarray.h warning suppression by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([#14496](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14496))
- **Compiler bug fix** for string operation miscompilation merged from [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) after extended review period ([#14010](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14010))
- **Stdlib documentation** merged with design guidelines from [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) after community discussion ([#14459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14459))
- Two PRs **closed without merging** including field getter syntax proposal by [Malek Ben Romdhane](https://github.com/malekbr) ([#14042](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14042)) and earlier ocamltest negation draft by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) superseded by [#14502](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14502) ([#13315](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13315))

- **Stdlib governance** formalization through documented design guidelines establishing principles for future development ([#14459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14459))

repositories.brief_summary: Focus on cmdliner compatibility with PRs for upgrading to version 2.0 and discussions on improving opam init experience
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs opened for cmdliner 2.0 compatibility and license field support, plus discussions on init workflow improvements
repositories.activity:
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) opened PR for **cmdliner 2.0 source compatibility** enabling future upgrades while maintaining current vendored version 1.3.0 ([#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854)), with code changes allowing compilation under cmdliner 2.0 and progressive enhancement opportunities including PowerShell completion support
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened draft PR to **improve `opam switch create` guidance** when `opam init` fails to create default switch ([#6851](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6851)), addressing confusion reported by new users in initialization failure scenarios
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) continued work on adding **license field to `opam list`** command ([#6841](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6841)), which [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noted will be useful for Docker and moby/vpnkit license generation workflows
repositories.discussions_summary: Extensive discussion on improving opam init UX and addressing cmdliner upgrade path
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Cmdliner upgrade path** discussed across multiple issues with [Josh Berdine](https://github.com/jberdine) requesting fixes from upstream cmdliner missing in opam's internal copy ([#6849](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6849)), [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) suggesting vendoring cmdliner 2.0 with legacy prefix patch to benefit from ANSI styling and improved error messages, and [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) volunteering to handle upgrade work including **PowerShell completion** support ([#6853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6853))
- **First-time user experience** extensively debated after [@przemmeq0](https://github.com/przemmeq0) reported that `opam init` silently exits when default switch creation fails and cleanup is confirmed ([#6848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6848)), with detailed analysis showing the note message was unclear and the command `opam switch create <compiler>` should be `opam switch create default 'ocaml>=4.05.0'` to match default switch behavior, leading [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to update related issue about making `opam init` easier to use ([#4821](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4821))
- **Repository origin visibility** proposed by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to display which repository a package comes from when multiple repositories are configured ([#6852](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6852)), addressing confusion from repository ranking behavior
- **Package removal and restore behavior** discussed by [Will Thomas](https://github.com/Durbatuluk1701) who found `opam remove` leaves packages as "restorable" with no clear way to prevent restoration ([#6850](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6850)), discovering packages remain in `.opam-switch/switch-state` roots section even after removal, with [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) referencing related restore experience issues ([#6622](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6622), [#6569](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6569))
- **Windows installation failure** reported by [@WellDoneYouWin](https://github.com/WellDoneYouWin) with curl.exe SSL certificate error during repository download ([#6847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6847)), with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) explaining Windows Schannel SSL support and requesting verbose curl output for debugging
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on Windows platform support improvements and new user onboarding experience
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Platform compatibility improvements** with Windows-specific work on cmdliner for PowerShell completion and SSL certificate troubleshooting, plus continued attention to cross-platform build directory cleanup affecting tools like opam-health-check-ng ([#5884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5884))
- **New user onboarding refinement** through better error messages, clearer documentation on switch creation commands, and addressing confusing initialization workflows when compiler installation fails
- **Cmdliner modernization** emerging as multi-faceted effort spanning bug fixes, Windows feature support, and upgrade path planning to benefit from improvements in cmdliner 2.0 while preserving opam's prefix specification requirements
repositories.contributors_summary: Active participation from Brian Ward, Kate, and community members with detailed feedback on initialization UX
repositories.contributors:
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) implemented cmdliner 2.0 source compatibility changes ([#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854)), opened feature request for PowerShell completion ([#6853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6853)), and volunteered to lead cmdliner upgrade work
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) created draft PR for improved switch creation guidance ([#6851](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6851)), opened issue on repository origin display ([#6852](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6852)), engaged in multiple discussions providing maintainer perspective on init behavior and cmdliner decisions, and reviewed cmdliner compatibility PR
- [Josh Berdine](https://github.com/jberdine) opened issue about cmdliner lagging behind upstream ([#6849](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6849))
- [@przemmeq0](https://github.com/przemmeq0) provided extensive analysis of first-time installation experience with detailed reproduction steps and constructive feedback on init workflow ([#6848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6848))
- [Will Thomas](https://github.com/Durbatuluk1701) investigated package removal and restore behavior ([#6850](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6850))
- [@WellDoneYouWin](https://github.com/WellDoneYouWin) reported Windows installation curl SSL error ([#6847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6847))
- [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) contributed technical guidance on cmdliner upgrade path, SSL certificate handling, and participated in restore behavior discussion
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) provided Windows SSL troubleshooting expertise and curl debugging guidance ([#6847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6847))
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) continued development of license field support ([#6841](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6841))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) commented on license field use case for Docker workflows ([#6841](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6841))
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) assigned as reviewer for cmdliner compatibility PR ([#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854))

repositories.brief_summary: New destruct custom request merged; three PRs remain under review for custom requests and configuration
repositories.new_features_summary: Destruct custom request added for case analysis and pattern matching generation
repositories.new_features: - **Destruct custom request** (`ocamllsp/destruct`) enables case analysis to generate or complete pattern matchings via a new API endpoint, supporting Merlin's case analysis functionality for improved code generation workflows ([#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583))
repositories.activity_summary: Three new PRs opened for locate types, signature help formatting, and merlin config server; one PR merged for destruct
repositories.activity:
- **Custom request for locate types** opened by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) to add `merlin-locate-types` functionality with compact representation of reachable types, currently under discussion regarding the necessity of the `set` field for client-side type handling ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584))
- **Signature help readability improvements** proposed by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) to apply ocamlformat to long signatures for better presentation, receiving review feedback about code clarity and naming conventions ([#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580))
- **Merlin configuration server flexibility** contributed by [Andrey Popp](https://github.com/andreypopp) to support custom build systems beyond Dune via environment variables `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` and `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT`, enabling alternative merlin config servers ([#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581))
- **Destruct custom request merged** by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) and reviewed by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), adding the `ocamllsp/destruct` endpoint for pattern matching generation with complete documentation and test coverage ([#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583))
- **Automated flake.lock update** by GitHub Actions updating Merlin and nixpkgs dependencies remains open, tracking latest upstream versions ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437))
repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussions on custom request API surface and client-side data handling complexity
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Type representation in locate types API** discussed between [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) regarding whether the compact `set` field provides value or adds unnecessary protocol complexity, with focus on balancing client-side convenience against API simplicity ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584))
- **Code review feedback on signature help** from [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) suggesting improved variable naming and code structure for the signature formatting parser, emphasizing readability and maintainability ([#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580))
repositories.trends_summary: Expansion of custom LSP requests to expose more Merlin capabilities directly to editor clients
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Custom request API expansion** continues with multiple PRs adding direct access to Merlin features like destruct, locate types, and type expression queries, moving functionality from code actions to explicit custom requests for better editor integration ([#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583), [#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584))
- **Build system flexibility** emerging as a theme with work to support merlin configuration servers beyond Dune, enabling integration with alternative OCaml build tools through environment-based configuration ([#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581))
- **UI/UX improvements for editor features** focused on presentation quality, including signature help formatting and type information display to enhance developer experience ([#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580))
repositories.contributors_summary: Xavier Van de Woestyne, Ulysse, Charlène_Gros, and Andrey Popp contributed code and reviews
repositories.contributors:
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) implemented the destruct custom request ([#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)) and opened the locate types custom request PR ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584)), contributing to the expansion of Merlin-backed editor capabilities
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) provided comprehensive code reviews across multiple PRs including destruct implementation ([#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)), signature help improvements ([#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580)), and locate types design ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584)), focusing on API design and code quality
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) contributed signature help readability improvements by applying ocamlformat to long function signatures ([#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580))
- [Andrey Popp](https://github.com/andreypopp) proposed merlin configuration server flexibility to support custom build systems through environment variables ([#1581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1581))

repositories.brief_summary: Test coverage improvements with four new test cases merged and signature help fix
repositories.new_features_summary: Signature help no longer loops parameters after completion; improved locate-types with Other variant support
repositories.new_features:
- **Signature help** now stops after the last parameter instead of looping back to the first parameter, fixing confusing behavior when too many arguments are provided ([#2023](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2023))
- **Locate-types result** now includes an `Other` variant for type expressions that don't fit other categories like arrows, tuples, objects, or type references, providing more complete type tree representations ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025))
- **Optional parameter handling** in locate-types improved by stripping the wrapping `option` type from optional parameters, showing the actual parameter type instead ([#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027))
- **Record field autocompletion** fixed to properly suggest field names when completing record expressions ([#2028](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2028))
- **PPX-based test infrastructure** repaired by adding environment directive and dependency configuration, allowing tests using ppx rewriters to run correctly again ([#2030](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2030))
repositories.activity_summary: One new PR for enclosing command region support; ongoing dependency updates and OCamlformat upgrade
repositories.activity:
- **New `-stop` flag proposal** for the `enclosing` command by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) to support region-based selections rather than just point positions, enabling better type annotation and locate-types behavior when selecting code fragments ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- **Test coverage expansion** with [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) adding reproduction cases for historical issues, confirming that [#1214](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1214) and [#1763](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1763) have been resolved ([#2022](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2022), [#2021](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2021), [#2020](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2020))
- **Test infrastructure improvements** by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) fixing PPX-using tests and adding regression test for Reason syntax handling ([#2030](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2030))
- **OCamlformat upgrade** to version 0.28.1 in progress with new PR opened ([#2031](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2031)), replacing earlier conflicting attempts ([#1984](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1984), [#1985](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1985))
- **GitHub Actions dependency updates** from [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) pending for actions/cache (v4→v5), actions/upload-artifact (v4→v6), actions/download-artifact (v5→v7), and cachix/install-nix-action (v31.8.4→v31.9.0), all updating to Node.js 24 runtime ([#2013](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2013), [#2014](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2014), [#2015](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2015), [#2012](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2012))
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on `-stop` flag implementation for region-based enclosing command
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Region-based code selection** discussion around the proposed `-stop` flag for the `enclosing` command, with [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) explaining that LSP's point-based positions are insufficient for OCaml's expression-heavy structure, particularly for type annotation and locate-types on code fragments ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- **Reason syntax type-enclosing issue** investigation where [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) reported that `ocamlmerlin single type-enclosing` stopped producing Reason syntax in OCaml 5.3+, but later confirmed they could no longer reproduce the issue, suggesting it was a configuration problem ([#2019](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2019))
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on improving test coverage and closing historical issues; modernizing CI infrastructure
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Systematic test coverage** initiative with multiple reproduction cases added for historical bug reports, helping identify which issues have been resolved and which remain problematic ([#2022](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2022), [#2021](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2021), [#2020](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2020))
- **Type system tooling refinement** with multiple improvements to locate-types functionality, making type navigation more comprehensive and accurate for editor integrations ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025), [#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027))
- **CI infrastructure modernization** as GitHub Actions dependencies migrate to Node.js 24 runtime, requiring coordinated updates across multiple action dependencies ([#2013](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2013), [#2014](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2014), [#2015](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2015), [#2012](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2012))
repositories.contributors_summary: Charlène_Gros focused on test coverage, Ulysse on test infrastructure and issue investigation, Xavier Van de Woestyne on enclosing command enhancement
repositories.contributors:

repositories.brief_summary: Package management CLI improvements, sandboxing enhancements, and OxCaml workspace support added
repositories.new_features_summary: New --pkg CLI flag for package management control and test sandboxing starting from dune 3.22
repositories.new_features:
- **Package management CLI control** via new `--pkg` flag enabling or disabling package management from command line, with `--pkg=enabled` and `--pkg=disabled` options ([#13458](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13458)) by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS)
- **Test sandboxing** enabled by default starting from dune 3.22, affecting test execution ([#13510](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13510)) and MDX tests from version 0.5 ([#13504](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13504)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **OxCaml workspace support** allowing `(using oxcaml 0.1)` stanza in workspace files ([#13395](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13395)) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)
- Fixed **promotion handling** allowing promotion into non-existent directories ([#13493](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13493)) and promoting files over directories when cram tests change from file to directory format ([#13516](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13516)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- Fixed **sandboxed action promotions** that previously disappeared when diff actions failed inside sandboxes ([#13520](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13520)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- Improved **sandbox cleanup** for sandboxes with modified permissions ([#13511](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13511)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- Fixed **file descriptor leak** on empty digest operations ([#13489](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13489)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- Fixed **promotion commands** to write output to stdout instead of stderr for `dune promotion list` ([#13462](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13462)) and `dune promotion show` ([#13481](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13481)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
repositories.activity_summary: Promotion refactoring, package management improvements, and ongoing OxCaml and Melange development
repositories.activity:
- **Package management enhancements** including new PRs for removing obsolete `--ignore-lock-dir` flag ([#13498](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13498)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) and ongoing work on odoc configuration file generation ([#13476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13476)), plus discussions on handling undefined package variables ([#13466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13466)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **Promotion system refactoring** with extensive cleanup work moving display logic to command line ([#13473](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13473), [#13482](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13482), [#13483](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13483)), removing unnecessary scheduler uses ([#13479](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13479)), and making promotion database abstract ([#13459](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13459)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Sandboxing refactoring** with PRs making sandbox arguments explicit in dependency evaluation ([#13508](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13508), [#13509](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13509), [#13503](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13503)) and using direct API for test rule creation ([#13505](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13505)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **OxCaml and Melange work** including ongoing PR for js_of_ocaml support for parameterized libraries ([#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414)) and new test showing Melange.emit doesn't respect `-p` flag ([#13521](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13521)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), plus merged OxCaml tutorial ([#12111](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12111))
- **Testing and CI improvements** with new Nix-based reverse dependency testing infrastructure ([#13222](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13222)) and test cases for package management edge cases ([#13464](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13464), [#13465](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13465)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **Infrastructure work** including directory traversal refactoring ([#13448](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13448)), moving Global_lock to Stdune ([#13474](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13474)), removing unused library dependencies ([#13475](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13475)), and renaming CLAUDE.md to AGENTS.md for broader AI agent compatibility ([#13487](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13487)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Documentation updates** improving tracing documentation ([#13488](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13488)) and adding hacking guide entries for reverse dependency testing by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- Open **bug reports** including dependency cycle crash ([#13500](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13500)), install stanza ignored in dynamic_include ([#13492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13492)), and request for user-defined variables support ([#13501](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13501))
- Closed issues including cram test promotion error ([#4067](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4067)) and clarification on `@pkg-install` target for dependency caching ([#13506](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13506))
repositories.discussions_summary: Package management configuration, dev tools rework, and patch_back_source_tree promotion mechanism
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Package management flag semantics** with ongoing discussion about merging `--ignore-lock-dir` into `--pkg` flag ([#13468](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13468), [#13469](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13469), [#13472](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13472)) considering options like `--pkg=autolocking` versus removing the flag entirely, led by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
- **Dev tools rework** with draft design document for tool management functionality ([#13457](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13457)) and related issues around constraints ([#13036](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13036)) and ocamlformat integration ([#10688](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10688))
- **Sandbox promotion mechanism** discussion on revived `patch_back_source_tree` feature ([#13495](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13495)) for actions that modify dependencies, with debate about naming and whether to use mode versus sandbox parameter, featuring input from [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) and [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Odoc configuration** discussion on generating `odoc-config.sexp` files ([#13485](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13485)) with implementation work in [#13476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13476), addressing whether to use `:with-doc` dependencies and how to handle `:post` dependencies
- **Relocatable compiler support** ongoing work tracking OCaml 5.5 relocatable compiler integration ([#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229)) to eliminate toolchain rebuilds, with multiple merged PRs for build-id interpretation and symlink resolution
- **Test beautification** discussion on generating formatted test reports ([#3937](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3937)) with suggestion to use new event emission feature ([#13348](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13348)) for custom displays
repositories.trends_summary: Sandboxing adoption, package management UX refinement, and internal code quality improvements
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Sandboxing expansion** across different subsystems with test execution sandboxing from 3.22 ([#13510](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13510)), MDX sandboxing from 0.5 ([#13504](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13504)), and extensive sandboxing infrastructure refactoring ([#13508](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13508), [#13509](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13509), [#13503](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13503))
- **Package management UX improvements** with command-line controls ([#13458](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13458)), flag consolidation discussions ([#13498](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13498), [#13472](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13472)), and ongoing work on relocatable compiler support ([#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229)) to reduce rebuild friction
- **Promotion system modernization** with command-line architecture cleanup ([#13473](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13473), [#13482](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13482)), proper stdout handling ([#13462](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13462), [#13481](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13481)), and new sandboxed promotion mechanisms ([#13520](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13520), [#13495](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13495))
- **OxCaml integration progress** with workspace file support ([#13395](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13395)), parameterized library tutorials ([#12111](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12111)), and ongoing js_of_ocaml compatibility work ([#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414))
- **Code quality focus** with internal refactoring for directory traversals ([#13448](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13448)), library dependency cleanup ([#13475](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13475)), and consistent error handling patterns
- **Testing infrastructure improvements** with Nix-based reverse dependency testing ([#13222](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13222)) and comprehensive test coverage for edge cases ([#13464](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13464), [#13465](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13465), [#13512](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13512))

repositories.brief_summary: Updated dune version requirement and opened three PRs addressing OxCaml support, type substitution bug, and URL remapping
repositories.new_features_summary: Updated minimum dune version to 3.21 with cram test renaming to comply with empty alias requirement
repositories.new_features: - Updated minimum **dune version requirement** from 3.18 to 3.21 ([#1398](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1398)) with **cram test file renaming** from `empty.t` to `empty_project.t` to comply with dune 3.20+ requirement that the `@empty` alias must be empty, merged by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) ([c3f0f46](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/commit/c3f0f46ee2cd1fef030764e0a76d387e889fd9e7))
repositories.activity_summary: Three new PRs opened for OxCaml compiler support, type substitution crash fix, and page reference URL remapping
repositories.activity:
- **OxCaml compiler support** PR opened by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) rebasing [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer)'s patches from oxcaml/odoc, leveraging dune 3.21's new `%{ocaml-config:ox}` variable to detect OxCaml compiler ([#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)), builds on the merged dune 3.21 update ([#1398](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1398))
- **Type substitution crash fix** PR opened by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) addressing [#930](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/930) which caused `Invalid_argument("List.fold_left2")` crashes when substituting for the same name at different arities ([#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1400)), with extensive edge case testing
- **URL remapping bug fix** for page references by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) resolving issue where page links weren't remapped due to trailing slash mismatch in prefix checking ([#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1395)), now includes filename in path matching and normalizes remap entries at config creation
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on cppo variable limitations for boolean values in dune configuration
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) noted challenges with **cppo conditional preprocessing** in dune, as cppo only accepts integer values in conditionals but dune's `%{ocaml-config:ox}` returns a boolean with no casting mechanism ([#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)), leading to rules duplication in build configuration
- [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) provided updated minimal working example for the **type substitution crash** ([#930](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/930)), demonstrating the issue now works through both `.cmt` and `.cmti` files with a different pattern involving destructive substitution
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on toolchain compatibility with dune and OxCaml compiler integration alongside core type system bug fixes
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Toolchain modernization** with adoption of dune 3.21 features and infrastructure updates to support multiple OCaml compiler variants
- **OxCaml compiler integration** efforts bringing experimental compiler support into upstream odoc through conditional compilation and AST version handling ([#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399))
- **Type system robustness** improvements addressing edge cases in type substitution and destructive substitution handling ([#930](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/930), [#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1400))
- **URL generation correctness** fixes for cross-package documentation references, particularly around page vs module link handling ([#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1395))
repositories.contributors_summary: Jon Ludlam opened two PRs for bug fixes, art-w merged dune update and opened OxCaml support PR, Luke Maurer provided testing insights
repositories.contributors:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) merged the **dune 3.21 update** ([#1398](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1398)) and opened the **OxCaml upstreaming PR** ([#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)) rebasing [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer)'s patches with fixes, and discussed cppo preprocessing limitations
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) opened PRs fixing the **type substitution crash** ([#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1400)) with comprehensive edge case testing and the **URL remapping bug** for page references ([#1395](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1395)), and enabled `%{ocaml-config:ox}` support in dune 3.21
- [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) provided updated **minimal working example** for the type substitution crash ([#930](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/930)) and contributed original OxCaml patches being upstreamed

repositories.trends_summary: Focus on documentation infrastructure and governance transparency improvements
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Documentation infrastructure improvements** are becoming a priority with work on explaining external documentation generation ([#3470](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3470)), improving error messages for missing docs ([#3471](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3471)), and establishing clear issue reporting paths to [ocaml-docs-ci](https://github.com/ocurrent/ocaml-docs-ci)

repositories.discussions_summary: Infrastructure improvements and policy discussions on opam-2.0 deprecation and OCaml compiler version support
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **CI infrastructure** issue resolved where Docker manifest inspection timeouts caused "No job variant" errors across multiple workflows ([#29296](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29296)), explained by [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) as 504 Gateway Timeouts during periodic SHA polling, with jobs successfully retried
- **opam-2.0 deprecation** discussion ongoing with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noting the team is moving toward deprecating opam 2.0.x in favor of 2.1+ ([#28952](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/28952), [discuss.ocaml.org](https://discuss.ocaml.org/t/proposal-make-the-minimum-tested-opam-2-1-and-higher/17736))
- **OCaml compiler support policy** discussion initiated by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) proposing to narrow supported versions to OCaml 4.14+ and 5.3+ from current 4.08.1-5.4.0 range ([#29059](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29059)), with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noting Debian 11 LTS support ending August 2026 (OCaml 4.11.1) as reasonable minimum floor
repositories.trends_summary: Ecosystem growth with diverse new packages and active compatibility management for OCaml 5.4.0 and cmdliner 2.0.0 transitions
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.4.0 preparation** intensifying with multiple upper bound additions indicating breaking AST changes, particularly affecting Longident types and parser-related packages requiring coordination across the ecosystem
- **cmdliner 2.0.0 migration** progressing with major packages like `dune-release` 2.2.0 and `capnp-rpc` 2.1.1 updating to support the new API, signaling broader ecosystem transition
- **Terminal UI development** gaining traction with new framework **mosaic** providing React-inspired hooks and The Elm Architecture, alongside **domainpc** for multicore programming, indicating growing interest in modern UI patterns
- **Async I/O ecosystem** evolution continuing with Lwt 6.1.0 addressing bugs, compatibility fixes for `inotify`, and related package updates showing active maintenance of concurrent programming infrastructure
- **Parser and formal methods** tooling advancing with menhir 20260122 release, menhir-lsp improvements, fstar source package distribution, and frama-c updates demonstrating ongoing investment in verification tools
- **Package constraint management** becoming more sophisticated with proactive compatibility work by maintainers, particularly [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) addressing version conflicts before they impact users
- **New package diversity** spanning web APIs (`pxshot`), embedded systems (`intel_hex`), React hooks validation (`react-rules-of-hooks-ppx`), and data structures (`mset`, `patricia-tree`) showing healthy ecosystem expansion across domains

repositories.brief_summary: JavaScript parser improvements, runtime eval enhancements, comprehensive manual rewrite, and loop miscompilation fix merged
repositories.new_features_summary: Decorator and HTML comment parser support, improved eval security with indirect eval and strict mode, loop exception handler fix
repositories.new_features:
- **JavaScript parser** now supports **decorators** for class and method syntax ([21da6678](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/21da6678c28134a6d5011c1914faa3a0de669c9b))
- **JavaScript parser** gained support for **HTML comments** in scripts, enabling compatibility with legacy web content ([87b7ae83](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/87b7ae83eee4ab723e1f7662756eed2694fce077))
- **Runtime eval functions** now use **indirect eval with optional chaining** (`eval?.()`) and prepend `"use strict"` directive for improved security and performance ([840e5a31](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/840e5a317cd7e5f9d9acbfc9fefa763d1b299bab), [#2108](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2108))
- **JavaScript parser** improvements include explicit **script vs module parsing modes** and better **await/yield normalization** ([fa7e63b7](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/fa7e63b7eac0b24d97957451254ecf3816957517), [20a190fd](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/20a190fd0546b18e954777d4edf171acd6735228))
- Fixed **miscompilation of loops at start of exception handlers** that could cause incorrect code generation ([b0cece2f](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/b0cece2fa6ed88f7d5a8b942b1716d0d5386d92d), [#2151](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2151))
- **Comprehensive manual rewrite** with new quickstart guide, JavaScript interop consolidation, syntax reference, error handling documentation, and improved API linking ([0cd5030a](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/0cd5030abb411ee5d88ee8b0b0d66c1f3ceab47e), [#2150](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2150))
- Added **CI job** to continuously test JavaScript parser against tc39/test262 test suite for standards compliance ([4bff921a](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4bff921a0f554aba190c3e49966748a23905399d), [#2149](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2149))
repositories.activity_summary: Three PRs opened for static evaluation experiments, 6.3 release preparation, and static evaluation; wasm runtime and manual documentation work completed
repositories.activity:
- **Static evaluation experiment** PR opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) exploring compile-time optimization opportunities ([#2153](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2153))
- **Release preparation** for version 6.3 opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) addressing pending PRs and runtime value re-export ([#2152](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2152))
- **Wasm runtime eval** functionality merged providing JavaScript eval function access in WebAssembly runtime after review discussions ([#2108](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2108))
- **Manual documentation** underwent substantial rewrite adding quickstart guide, consolidated JavaScript interop documentation, error handling guide, syntax reference, improved options and PPX documentation, plus new runtime representation guide ([#2150](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2150))
- **JavaScript parser** test infrastructure enhanced with tc39/test262 integration, test flexibility improvements, and recovery strategy enhancements ([#2149](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2149))
- **Popover API implementation** continues with review addressing property capitalization, options handling, and specification updates by [Sylvain Boilard](https://github.com/SylvainBoilard) ([#1734](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1734))
- **Ongoing draft work** includes JavaScript string implementation for Wasm ([#1772](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1772)), function/block parameter unboxing ([#1953](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1953)), primitive inlining ([#1928](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1928)), exception handling via null returns ([#1910](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1910)), Stack Switching proposal effects ([#1832](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1832)), WASI runtime ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), and Oxcaml support ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105))
- **Performance optimizations** awaiting review include aggressive lambda lifting ([#1886](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1886)), bigstring operations ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)), string/arraybuffer conversions ([#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124)), and exception handling ([#2098](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2098))
- **Library enhancements** in progress include CSS Font Loading API wrapper by [Mesmera (WAFT)](https://github.com/mesmerizingwaft) ([#1833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1833)) and Intl.RelativeTimeFormat API by [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) ([#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070))

repositories.trends_summary: Compiler robustness focus through parser modernization, runtime security hardening, and comprehensive documentation improvements
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **JavaScript parser modernization** continues with modern ECMAScript feature support including decorators, HTML comments, script/module modes, and continuous validation against official tc39 test suite
- **Runtime security improvements** through indirect eval usage, strict mode enforcement, and safer evaluation practices addressing potential variable capture issues
- **Documentation quality** receiving focused attention with comprehensive manual restructuring, new contributor guides, API reference improvements, and better cross-linking
- **WebAssembly runtime maturity** progressing with Stack Switching proposal implementation, WASI runtime development, JavaScript string integration experiments, and multiple optimization efforts
- **Performance optimization research** exploring lambda lifting strategies, unboxing opportunities, exception handling alternatives, and type precision improvements for Wasm target
- **Standards compliance** emphasized through test262 integration and systematic validation against ECMAScript specifications

repositories.brief_summary: ARM64 binary emitter shipped with verification infrastructure, plus new unboxed unit type and syntax
repositories.new_features_summary: ARM64 binary emitter, `#()` literals for unboxed unit, `unit#` type, `%domain_index` primitive, improved Gc.stat
repositories.new_features:
- Binary **machine code emitter for ARM64** architecture by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell), replacing text-based assembly generation with direct binary output for JIT compilation ([#5177](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5177), [#5340](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5340), [#5188](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5188))
- Binary emitter **verification infrastructure** by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) with new `-verify-binary-emitter` flag that compares generated binary output against system assembler for correctness validation ([#5198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5198), [#5345](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5345))
- **Unboxed unit literal syntax** `#()` added by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) for zero-sized unboxed tuple representation ([#5156](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5156))
- Abstract **`unit#` type** of kind `void mod everything` introduced by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) as unboxed unit variant created with `%unbox_unit` primitive ([#5151](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5151))
- New **`%domain_index` primitive** by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) implements `Domain.self_index` directly in the compiler for efficient domain identification ([#5312](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5312))
- **Improved `Gc.stat` and `Gc.quick_stat`** reporting by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) with runtime 5 support for `heap_chunks` field and better documentation of cost differences between functions ([#5226](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5226))
- **64-bit EH frame support** for dissector by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) with new `-dissector-assume-lld-without-64-bit-eh-frames` and `-no-dissector-assume-lld-without-64-bit-eh-frames` command-line flags ([#5303](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5303))
- Support for **arrays of unboxed pairs of vec128s** by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) enabling vec128 & vec128 and vec128 & vec128 & vec128 & vec128 array types for SIMD operations ([#5239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5239))
- **Block indices optimization** for mixed products by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) using 52-bit offset and 12-bit gap with bumped mixed block version ([#5324](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5324))
- **Debug location tracking** improvements for partially-applied functions by [@shym](https://github.com/shym) enabling proper DWARF information generation ([#5225](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5225))
- **Layout polymorphism** and `or_null` support added to `Stdlib_stable.IarrayLabels` by [David Vulakh](https://github.com/dvulakh) with proper function labelling ([#5309](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5309))
- **Builtin primitives** now support more than 5 arguments without requiring `native_name` by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) ([#5326](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5326))
- **Small integer conversions** expanded by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) for better numeric type interoperability ([#5305](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5305))
- **ARM64 large stack offsets** bug fix by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) addressing issue #4511 ([#5130](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5130))
- **Type inspection** for polymorphic applications under quotes by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) covering record fields, object methods, and annotated functions ([#5154](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5154), [#5214](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5214))
- **GDB utility improvements** to `memsearch.py` for better address space searching by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([#4886](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4886))
repositories.activity_summary: 24 new PRs opened including type system work, backend improvements, and upstream merge efforts

repositories.brief_summary: Added kind-enclosing query with verbosity control, improved locate-types and record field completion
repositories.new_features_summary: New kind-enclosing query, enhanced verbosity display, improved locate-types and record field completion
repositories.new_features:
- New **kind-enclosing query** command added as jkind analogue of type-enclosing hovers by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), enabling developers to view kinds at cursor position ([#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208))
- Enhanced **verbosity control** for kind-enclosing query with `override_verbosity` option allowing custom verbosity levels independent of main Mconfig settings ([#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208))
- Improved **verbosity display** for kinds with three levels: verbosity 0 shows simplified format, verbosity 1 shows expanded format, and verbosity 2 shows expanded format with all modality bounds including internal and non_null axes ([#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208))
- Fixed **record field autocompletion** by cherry-picking upstream changes addressing completion issues ([#209](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/209), [ocaml/merlin#2028](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2028))
- Enhanced **locate-types command** to include variables in results and properly handle optional function parameters by stripping option types from optional params ([#207](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/207), [ocaml/merlin#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025), [ocaml/merlin#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027))
- Improved **type tree representation** in locate-types by including all type components instead of filtering, and adding `Other` node type for types that don't match specific patterns ([#207](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/207))
- **OxCaml compiler integration** with merge of 5.2.0minus-29 bringing typing system updates, jkind refactoring, mode system improvements, and language extension changes across 195 files ([#212](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/212))
repositories.activity_summary: Three PRs remain open including signature help refinement and mode-enclosing query, plus ongoing compiler integration work
repositories.activity:
- Three **new PRs opened** this week including signature help refinement by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) to prevent signature help appearing on function names ([#206](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/206)), mode-enclosing query implementation by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) as mode analogue of type hovers ([#210](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/210)), and draft OxCaml merge PR ([#211](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/211))
- **Kind-enclosing query** merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) after review by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js), adding jkind hover support with verbosity control and comprehensive test coverage ([#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208))
- **Locate-types improvements** merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) cherry-picking two upstream PRs to include variables in results and handle optional function parameters correctly ([#207](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/207))
- **Record field completion fix** merged by [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) importing changes from upstream to address autocompletion issues ([#209](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/209))
- **OxCaml 5.2.0minus-29 merge** completed by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) importing compiler changes including typing system refactoring, jkind simplification, mode system restructuring, and environment handling updates ([#212](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/212))
- **Conflict resolution** completed by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) fixing merge conflict left in comment from minus-29 merge ([#213](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/213))
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on editor tooling improvements with hover queries and enhanced type inspection capabilities
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Development focused on **editor tooling enhancements** with new hover capabilities including kind-enclosing ([#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208)) and planned mode-enclosing ([#210](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/210)) queries providing more type system information at cursor position
- Continued **upstream integration** pattern with cherry-picking improvements from [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) including locate-types enhancements ([#207](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/207)) and record field completion fixes ([#209](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/209))
- **OxCaml compiler synchronization** remains active with ongoing merge work bringing typing system changes, jkind refactoring, and mode system improvements to Merlin ([#212](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/212), [#211](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/211))
- Enhanced **verbosity control** emerging as theme with customizable display levels for kind information allowing developers to adjust detail level for different contexts ([#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208))
- **Type navigation improvements** with better locate-types results including variables and proper handling of optional parameters making jump-to-definition more comprehensive ([#207](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/207))
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson led development with four merged PRs and dkalinichenko-js provided reviews while Kostia Balytskyi contributed fixes
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) implemented kind-enclosing query with verbosity control ([#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208)), cherry-picked locate-types improvements ([#207](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/207)), merged OxCaml 5.2.0minus-29 changes ([#212](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/212)), resolved merge conflicts ([#213](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/213)), and opened mode-enclosing query PR ([#210](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/210))
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) provided code reviews for kind-enclosing query including feedback on duplicate entries, missing options, and documentation phrasing ([#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208)) and for mode-enclosing query ([#210](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/210))
- [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) imported upstream record field completion fixes addressing autocompletion issues ([#209](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/209))
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) opened PR to fix signature help behavior preventing it from appearing on function names ([#206](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/206))

repositories.brief_summary: Added opam-core and opam-format packages with locality fixes; expanded CI testing to include odoc and sherlodoc
repositories.new_features_summary: Added opam-core and opam-format packages with locality type conversion patches for OxCaml compatibility
repositories.new_features:
- **New opam-core 2.5.0+ox package** with patches to fix locality type conversion issues where `fn : _ @ local -> _` expressions now work with explicit lambda wrappers `(fun x -> fn x)` for proper `@ global` casting ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
- **New opam-format 2.5.0+ox package** added as dependency for opam tooling, enabling proper format library support for opam 2.5 under OxCaml ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
- **CI testing expanded** to include odoc and sherlodoc packages in the automated installation verification workflow ([#34](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/34))
repositories.activity_summary: Fixed opam-core compilation errors and expanded CI coverage for documentation tools
repositories.activity:
- **Locality type conversion patches** applied to opam-core 2.5.0+ox addressing compilation failures where locality-annotated functions (`_ @ local -> _`) were not automatically cast to global context (`_ @ global -> _`), requiring explicit lambda wrappers as a quickfix by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
- **Documentation toolchain support** improved with [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) adding odoc and sherlodoc to CI package testing, resolving installation verification for oxcaml/odoc#22 compilation errors ([#34](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/34))
- **Upstream contribution discussion** initiated by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggesting these locality fixes should be fed back to upstream opam as quality of life improvements, similar to recent [ocaml/dune#13342](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13342) changes ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued pattern of adapting OCaml ecosystem packages for OxCaml's locality type system with explicit conversion workarounds
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Locality type system integration** continues to require patches across the OCaml ecosystem, with opam-core joining the growing list of packages needing explicit type conversion workarounds for functions with locality annotations
- **Upstream contribution opportunities** emerging as OxCaml-specific fixes (like locality type conversions) could benefit the broader OCaml ecosystem when generalized, following the pattern established with the dune project
- **CI-driven quality assurance** expanding to cover documentation tooling (odoc, sherlodoc) alongside core development tools, ensuring the OxCaml fork maintains compatibility with the full OCaml toolchain
repositories.contributors_summary: Two contributors merged PRs with art-w fixing opam packages and mt_caret expanding CI testing; avsm provided reviews
repositories.contributors:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) implemented locality type conversion patches for opam-core and opam-format packages, creating new 2.5.0+ox versions with explicit lambda wrapper workarounds for global/local casting issues ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
- [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) expanded the CI testing workflow to include odoc and sherlodoc in the automated package installation verification, addressing compilation errors in the documentation toolchain ([#34](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/34))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) reviewed both PRs, merged them to the main branch, and provided guidance on contributing locality fixes upstream to the OCaml ecosystem ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33), [#34](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/34))

group_summaries.brief_summary: 48 package releases merged with OCaml 5.4.0 compatibility work, plus governance updates and documentation infrastructure improvements

group_summaries.activity_summary: Major releases for Lwt 6.1.0, Menhir 20260122, and governance team updates across both repositories

- **Governance pages updated** with current team membership by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) adding Language Committee, Security Team, and Standard Library Team details in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3460](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3460), maintainer list refreshed by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) to reflect January 2026 status in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3467](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3467), and security team contacts improved by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3462](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3462)
